evidence: `.github/actions/GithubToken/generate_token.py` executes env validation, JWT creation, outbound HTTP, and output-file write at module import time (lines 10-56), so importing the module triggers external side effects.

`src/thenvoi/integrations/claude_sdk/session_manager.py` raises ImportError at module import if optional dependency is absent (lines 18-25), unlike `src/thenvoi/integrations/codex/websocket_client.py` which defers optional import to `connect()` (lines 40-45).

Test modules import helper APIs directly from conftest modules (`tests/runtime/test_presence.py` line 10, `tests/runtime/test_runtime.py` line 11, `tests/integration/test_smoke.py` line 8), coupling test files to central fixture hubs rather than local utilities.

Integration test entrypoint is a re-export facade (`tests/integration/conftest.py` lines 9-61) that exposes many symbols from another conftest module, expanding implicit public surface and making ownership boundaries unclear.

`tests/conftest.py` combines pytest collection policy (`pytest_ignore_collect`, lines 54-56), broad event factory surface (lines 64-190), and SDK agent/session fixtures (lines 198-258) in one high-fan-in module.

`examples/coding_agents/test_communication.py` mutates `sys.path` at import time (line 24) and embeds ad-hoc environment/config orchestration in one script, indicating boundary bypass instead of explicit package entrypoints.

Example logging helpers share the same name (`setup_logging`) but use conflicting root logger policies and formats: root WARNING + namespace override in `examples/a2a_bridge/setup_logging.py` and `examples/claude_sdk/setup_logging.py` vs root set directly to caller level in `examples/a2a_gateway/setup_logging.py` and `examples/codex/setup_logging.py`.

`examples/a2a_gateway/demo_orchestrator/__init__.py` uses try/except dual import fallback while sibling package `__init__.py` files are simple docstrings/re-exports, creating a style island for package loading behavior.

Two example tool packages are near-identical copies (`examples/claude_sdk_docker/tools/example_tools.py` and `examples/coding_agents/tools/example_tools.py`), which indicates convention drift in how reusable example scaffolding is organized.

`src/thenvoi/integrations/langgraph/langchain_tools.py` catches broad exceptions in nearly every wrapper and returns plain error strings (e.g., `"Error ..."`), while `src/thenvoi/integrations/langgraph/graph_tools.py` explicitly lets subgraph errors bubble.
`src/thenvoi/integrations/parlant/tools.py` reports failures as `ToolResult(data=...)` and logs exceptions, creating a third failure contract for the same conceptual tool surface.

Demo gateway path mixes raise-and-wrap with string-error returns: `examples/a2a_gateway/demo_orchestrator/remote_agent.py` raises `RuntimeError`, `agent.py` catches it and returns an error string, and `agent_executor.py` separately maps exceptions to `ServerError`.

The orchestrator handoff uses untyped dict payloads (`is_task_complete`, `require_user_input`, `content`) between `agent.py` and `agent_executor.py`, requiring fragile key-based choreography instead of a typed seam.

Tool-layer boundaries are inconsistent about whether failures are control-flow exceptions or successful string payloads, so orchestration code cannot rely on a single contract when composing integrations.
Large blocks of repetitive wrapper code in `langchain_tools.py` and repeated example tool modules indicate missing shared boundary abstractions for common tool-adapter handoffs.

src/thenvoi/adapters/crewai.py (1239 lines) and src/thenvoi/adapters/claude_sdk.py (1087 lines) each embed large tool-definition surfaces with repeated call/report/result patterns rather than sharing a tool adapter builder.
In CrewAI, many BaseTool classes differ mostly by tool name and argument plumbing; repeated _report_tool_call/_report_tool_result/json response scaffolding dominates behavior.
Claude SDK and CrewAI adapters both solve the same platform-tool bridging concern with separate bespoke abstractions, increasing indirection and maintenance cost.

`A2AGatewayAdapter` directly depends on REST client internals (`AsyncRestClient`, request DTOs) and manages room/context state itself rather than reusing runtime tool boundaries, creating a separate dependency path from the rest of adapters.
Peer resolution and mapping concerns are duplicated in both `A2AGatewayAdapter._resolve_peer` and `GatewayServer._resolve_peer`, so model/route changes require synchronized edits across modules.
Tests heavily assert private adapter state (`_context_to_room`, `_room_participants`, `_pending_tasks`) in gateway tests, indicating dependency on implementation details rather than stable seams.

`PydanticAIAdapter._create_agent()` and `langchain_tools.agent_tools_to_langchain()` contain long repetitive blocks of nearly identical wrapper functions, reducing local signal-to-noise and making targeted edits error-prone.
`ClaudeSDKAdapter._create_mcp_server()` similarly contains a large monolithic run of tool wrapper declarations with repeated parsing/error/result formatting boilerplate.

pyproject.toml excludes `thenvoi-bridge/**` from pyrefly checks, so production bridge contracts (including session lifecycle in `thenvoi-bridge/bridge_core/session.py`) are outside one of the default quality gates.
tests/integration/conftest.py is a re-export hub with a long mirrored import/export list, creating a drift-prone fixture surface that can fail silently when parent fixture contracts change.
tests/conftest.py centralizes many cross-domain fixtures and event factories, increasing coupling of unrelated tests to shared test internals.
All four scoped files are comment-only package markers with near-template phrasing (e.g., "# Tests for example agents", "# LangGraph example tests").
Comments restate folder/module names instead of adding intent, migration status, or package-level behavior.
No executable content or explanatory package contract exists in these __init__.py files, indicating boilerplate residue rather than purposeful module documentation.

holistic_context.structure shows `src/thenvoi/runtime/` has 15 files and `tests/runtime/` has 16 files, both above the >10 mixed-concern split threshold; contact-specific modules/tests are mixed with broader runtime lifecycle modules in the same flat folders.

A2A gateway tests are split across `tests/integration/test_a2a_gateway_context.py` and `tests/integrations/a2a/gateway/test_adapter.py` / `tests/integrations/a2a/gateway/test_server.py`, indicating domain-affinity mismatch and duplicated navigation paths for one subsystem.
`tests/integration/conftest.py` is a pure re-export shim over `tests/conftest_integration.py`, creating two ownership locations for integration fixture contracts.

Contact-management behavior is implemented twice with near-identical shapes in `src/thenvoi/runtime/tools.py` (room-bound `AgentTools` contact methods) and `src/thenvoi/runtime/contact_tools.py` (agent-level `ContactTools`), creating parallel maintenance paths.

Example agent scripts repeat the same startup pipeline (load env, validate URLs, load credentials, create adapter, create Agent, run) across multiple frameworks (for example `examples/anthropic/03_tom_agent.py`, `examples/crewai/05_tom_agent.py`, `examples/pydantic_ai/03_tom_agent.py`).

Infrastructure runner behavior is copy-pasted across runner entrypoints (`docker/claude_sdk/runner.py`, `docker/codex/runner.py`, `examples/claude_sdk_docker/runner.py`): mount validation, signal handling, config loading, and retry loop logic are reimplemented with local variation.

Cross-module event-shape handling is inconsistent: `src/thenvoi/runtime/formatters.py` and `src/thenvoi/runtime/tools.py` preserve `sender_type` as platform casing (e.g., `Agent`), while `src/thenvoi/converters/a2a_gateway.py` checks lowercase `agent` for participant tracking.

findings.summary: Multiple entrypoint-like modules execute side effects at import time, creating unsafe import boundaries and hidden execution coupling.
evidence: GitHub token script performs env validation, JWT signing, HTTP POST, and file write at top level (`generate_token.py` lines 10-56).
Example scripts call `setup_logging()` at module import (`02_with_auth.py` line 30, `01_basic_gateway.py` line 59).
Coding-agent script mutates interpreter import path at module import (`test_communication.py` line 24).
findings.suggestion: Standardize all these files as explicit entrypoints with `main()`/`if __name__ == '__main__'` boundaries and keep top-level code import-safe (definitions only). Move IO/network/env validation into runtime functions.

findings.summary: Claude integration hard-fails at module import when optional dependency is missing, violating optional-integration boundary isolation.
evidence: `session_manager.py` raises ImportError immediately during module import (lines 18-25).
`websocket_client.py` defers optional dependency import to connection-time (`connect()`, lines 40-45), allowing module import without package presence.
findings.suggestion: Mirror the Codex pattern: defer `claude_agent_sdk` import to runtime paths (`connect`/factory) and raise targeted runtime errors only when Claude integration is actually invoked.

findings.summary: Core runtime tests depend on a broad root conftest helper hub, amplifying blast radius for unrelated fixture changes.
evidence: `tests/conftest.py` hosts heterogeneous concerns (collection behavior, event builders, and agent/session fixtures; lines 54-258).
`test_presence.py` and `test_runtime.py` import helper constructors directly from `tests.conftest` (line 10 and line 11 respectively), creating explicit module dependencies on the hub.
findings.suggestion: Extract event-builder helpers into a dedicated test utility module (e.g., `tests/helpers/events.py`) and import from that utility; keep root `conftest.py` focused on fixture registration only.

findings.summary: Integration test setup uses a wide re-export facade that obscures ownership and creates an implicit API contract for tests.
evidence: `tests/integration/conftest.py` re-exports many fixtures/helpers via import list + `__all__` (lines 9-61).
`tests/integration/test_smoke.py` imports `requires_api` directly from that conftest facade (line 8), turning conftest into a consumed module API rather than discovery-only fixture registration.
findings.suggestion: Stop importing from conftest modules in tests; move reusable markers/helpers into explicit modules (e.g., `tests/integration/markers.py`) and keep `conftest.py` as thin pytest registration glue.

findings.summary: Coding-agent communication script bypasses package boundaries with runtime path injection and in-file orchestration, reducing structural clarity.
evidence: `test_communication.py` modifies `sys.path` directly (line 24) to resolve imports.
It mixes config loading, REST orchestration, timing waits, and reporting in one top-level script flow (lines 29-111), unlike cleaner adapter-driven entrypoint structure in `01_basic_gateway.py`.
findings.suggestion: Package coding-agent example utilities as importable modules and run via `python -m ...`; remove `sys.path` mutation and split orchestration steps into reusable functions (`load_config`, `create_room`, `invite_participants`, `assert_delivery`).

findings.summary: Tool adapters expose three incompatible failure contracts (exception, string error, ToolResult error).
evidence: LangChain wrappers catch `Exception` and return text like `"Error sending message: ..."` as normal tool output.
`graph_as_tool` documents and implements bubble-up exceptions instead of stringifying failures.
Parlant wrappers convert errors into `ToolResult(data=...)` while also logging, producing a separate contract shape.
findings.suggestion: Define a single cross-integration error envelope (for example `{ok: bool, data: ..., error: {code, message}}`) and adapt each integration to emit/consume that envelope consistently; only convert to framework-specific types at the outermost boundary.

findings.summary: Gateway call failures are downgraded to plain response text, obscuring real execution failure state.
evidence: `remote_agent.py` raises `RuntimeError` on call failure.
`agent.py` catches broad exception in `call_peer_agent` and returns `"Error calling peer ..."` string.
`agent_executor.py` treats only raised exceptions as protocol failures (`ServerError`), so many runtime failures become ordinary model-visible text.
findings.suggestion: Keep transport/runtime failures as structured exceptions through the tool seam and map them once in the executor to protocol-level error states; reserve normal string content for successful peer responses.

findings.summary: Executor-agent seam relies on ad-hoc dict keys instead of a typed response contract.
evidence: `OrchestratorAgent.stream()` yields raw dicts with string keys (`is_task_complete`, `require_user_input`, `content`).
`OrchestratorAgentExecutor.execute()` indexes those keys directly and branches control flow on them.
A typo or shape change would fail at runtime and is not protected by a shared typed model.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a shared typed payload model/dataclass for stream events (status enum + content + optional metadata) and use that model in both producer and consumer to make handoffs explicit and safer.

findings.summary: Same helper name (`setup_logging`) implements conflicting root logger conventions across examples.
evidence: Some modules pin root logger to WARNING and then tune package loggers (`a2a_bridge`, `claude_sdk`, `parlant`).
Others set root logger directly from function argument (`a2a_gateway`, `codex`) with different formatting and stream behavior.
This makes identical `setup_logging()` calls behave differently depending on example family.
findings.suggestion: Create one shared example logging helper (or documented policy) with explicit extension points per framework and have each example call that shared helper instead of defining local variants.

findings.summary: Two tool packages are duplicated verbatim, creating convention drift risk from copy-based reuse.
evidence: The calculator/time/random tool implementations and registry structure are effectively duplicated across both directories.
Future fixes to one example tool set can easily diverge from the other because there is no shared source of truth.
findings.suggestion: Extract shared example tools into a single reusable module and import from both example packages, or generate both from one template to enforce synchronized conventions.

findings.summary: Tool-surface abstraction is fragmented across adapters with repeated wrapper logic.
evidence: Each file defines its own `thenvoi_*` wrapper set that maps framework callback signatures to `AgentToolsProtocol` and catches exceptions into string/error payloads.
The wrapper sets duplicate behavior (argument extraction/defaulting, error formatting, tool description binding) but are implemented separately per framework.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a shared tool-binding factory (e.g., canonical tool spec + per-framework renderer) so each adapter only supplies framework-specific registration primitives; centralize error/result mapping in one reusable layer.

findings.summary: A2A gateway path creates a parallel dependency stack and duplicated peer-resolution logic.
evidence: `A2AGatewayAdapter` directly uses REST DTOs/clients and room bookkeeping instead of reusing runtime tool/session boundaries.
Both adapter and server implement their own peer resolution logic (`_resolve_peer`) over the same slug/UUID maps.
Gateway tests assert private state maps directly, showing call-site dependency on internal storage shape.
findings.suggestion: Extract a shared `GatewayDirectory`/`GatewaySessionStore` boundary used by both adapter and server, and expose testable public methods for context mapping so tests stop coupling to private dicts.

findings.summary: Room bootstrap/injection/cleanup choreography is repeated across adapters.
evidence: Multiple adapters implement near-identical flow: initialize per-room history on bootstrap, inject participants/contacts as synthetic user/system messages, append current message, then cleanup per room.
Differences are incidental (message container types), but lifecycle policy is duplicated, which risks drift when session semantics change.
findings.suggestion: Add a shared session-lifecycle helper in `SimpleAdapter` (or a mixin) that standardizes bootstrap, metadata injection, and cleanup hooks, with adapters only providing framework-specific message constructors.

findings.summary: Large monolithic tool-registration functions create local complexity and edit risk.
evidence: `_create_agent()` and `_create_mcp_server()` contain long sequences of mostly repetitive closures with duplicated try/except and schema/doc wiring.
Small behavioral changes to one tool family require touching many nearly identical blocks, increasing inconsistent edits.
findings.suggestion: Refactor to table-driven registration: declare tool metadata and target `AgentToolsProtocol` method once, then generate wrappers/registrations in loops with shared error handling helpers.

findings.summary: Critical JSON-RPC edge branches in rpc_base are not explicitly exercised by tests.
evidence: BaseJsonRpcClient._dispatch_rpc_message contains branch-specific behavior for malformed JSON, non-dict payloads, queue overflow drops, and unknown payloads.
Current codex transport tests emphasize initialize/request/notify/respond flows and overload retries; they do not target malformed input or queue saturation handling paths.
findings.suggestion: Add focused unit tests around BaseJsonRpcClient dispatch/failure behavior (malformed JSON, non-object payloads, queue-full for notifications/requests, unknown payload logging path) using a lightweight test subclass to inject messages deterministically.

findings.summary: BaseHandler contract lacks direct tests, leaving protocol drift detection to integration behavior.
evidence: BaseHandler is the canonical protocol surface for bridge integrations.
Router tests verify forwarded kwargs and routing outcomes, but there is no dedicated contract test suite validating protocol-level invariants/documented semantics in handler.py.
findings.suggestion: Create explicit protocol contract tests for the bridge handler seam (required fields, semantic expectations for sender/thread metadata, and compatibility checks for handler implementations) so API changes fail fast before runtime integration tests.

findings.summary: Handler boundary is a wide primitive parameter bag, increasing glue-code entropy.
evidence: BaseHandler.handle currently requires many scalar fields plus tools, rather than a cohesive request/context object.
MentionRouter.route constructs and forwards each field manually, binding transport/message schema details directly into the handler call seam.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a typed `MentionContext` (or equivalent Pydantic/dataclass model) that encapsulates message metadata and sender identity, and update `BaseHandler.handle` to accept `(context, tools)` to centralize boundary translation and reduce synchronized call-site churn.

findings.summary: Comment-only __init__.py markers are repeated boilerplate with no package-level value
evidence: The files only contain one-line labels like "# Tests for A2A Gateway example agents".
The text mirrors directory names and does not document why these packages exist or what they expose.
Pattern is duplicated across all assigned files, indicating generated/template residue.
findings.suggestion: Either remove these __init__.py files if package markers are unnecessary for test discovery, or replace each with concise module docstrings that state package purpose and any migration/deprecation context.

findings.summary: Init files provide no explicit contract and add repetitive low-signal noise
evidence: No file defines package exports, constants, or meaningful package documentation.
Single-line comments are generic and not actionable for readers changing test package boundaries.
Sibling files follow the same minimal placeholder pattern, showing uncurated local craft.
findings.suggestion: Standardize these package entry files: either keep them empty (no comment) where no contract is needed, or add a consistent short docstring template that declares role, scope, and any migration status.

findings.summary: A2A gateway tests are split between generic integration and domain-specific integration trees.
evidence: `tests/integration/test_a2a_gateway_context.py` exercises `A2AGatewayAdapter` directly, but sibling A2A gateway tests live under `tests/integrations/a2a/gateway/`.
The structure profile shows a dedicated `tests/integrations/a2a/gateway/` package already exists, so the root integration placement is an outlier rather than a missing folder case.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) create/confirm target folder `tests/integrations/a2a/gateway/`; (2) move `tests/integration/test_a2a_gateway_context.py` to `tests/integrations/a2a/gateway/test_context_flow.py`; (3) if needed, add a local `conftest.py` in that folder that imports shared fixtures from `tests/conftest_integration.py`; (4) run import updates with `rg -n "test_a2a_gateway_context|from \.conftest import requires_api" tests/` and adjust references; (5) validate with `uv run pytest tests/integrations/a2a/gateway -v` and `uv run pytest tests/integration -v`.

findings.summary: Runtime production/tests exceed cohesion thresholds and mix contact-specific and core runtime concerns in flat folders.
evidence: structure profile: `src/thenvoi/runtime/` has 15 files and `tests/runtime/` has 16 files (both >10), with mixed concerns (contact events, execution engine, prompts, shutdown, trackers).
Contact-specific files/tests are intermixed with unrelated runtime lifecycle modules/tests instead of being grouped under a focused contact boundary.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) create `src/thenvoi/runtime/contact/` and `tests/runtime/contact/`; (2) move `contact_handler.py` and `contact_tools.py` first, then move `test_broadcast_changes.py` and `test_contact_config.py`; (3) add thin compatibility imports in old module paths during transition (`from .contact.contact_handler import ...`) and remove after callers migrate; (4) update imports via `rg -n "thenvoi\.runtime\.contact_(handler|tools)|tests\.runtime\.test_contact" src tests` and patch callers; (5) validate with `uv run pytest tests/runtime/contact -v` then `uv run pytest tests/runtime -v`.

findings.summary: The runtime package’s top-level API and folder shape blur subsystem ownership.
evidence: `src/thenvoi/runtime/__init__.py` exports a broad, heterogeneous API surface (execution, presence, tools, prompts, trackers, shutdown), which dilutes explicit package role boundaries.
Contact event handling is implemented as dedicated modules and dedicated tests but not represented as a first-class subpackage boundary, so decomposition is discoverable only by reading file names.
findings.suggestion: Introduce an explicit runtime decomposition plan: (1) add subpackages `runtime/core/` (execution/presence/runtime lifecycle) and `runtime/contact/` (contact handler/tools/types adapters); (2) move contact modules first, then prune `runtime/__init__.py` to a minimal public contract; (3) if backward compatibility is needed, keep temporary re-exports with deprecation notes; (4) run `rg -n "from thenvoi\.runtime import|thenvoi\.runtime\.[a-z_]+" src tests` to update imports systematically; (5) validate with `uv run pytest tests/runtime -v` and `uv run pytest tests/integration/test_a2a_gateway_context.py -v`.

findings.summary: Core install path is coupled to an adapter-specific dependency despite optional-extra docs.
evidence: README.md instructs base install and framework-specific extras separately, implying integrations are opt-in.
pyproject.toml base `dependencies` includes `claude-agent-sdk>=0.1.16` while `claude_sdk` extra also declares `claude-agent-sdk>=0.1.0`.
findings.suggestion: Make adapter SDKs truly optional: move `claude-agent-sdk` from base `dependencies` to `project.optional-dependencies.claude_sdk`, keep base dependencies framework-agnostic, and align README install examples to one policy (base-only + explicit extras).

findings.summary: Top-level ownership is blurred between SDK core, integration adapters, and bridge runtime.
evidence: README describes a cohesive SDK with broad production-ready capability matrix.
Bridge runtime contracts (SessionData/SessionStore/InMemorySessionStore) are implemented outside `src/thenvoi/`, while package metadata and quality gates are centered on `src/thenvoi/`.
findings.suggestion: Define an explicit architecture contract section in README and enforce it in packaging: either fold bridge runtime under `src/thenvoi/bridge/` as an owned subsystem or declare it as a separate package/project with its own pyproject and independent CI gates.

findings.summary: Bridge production code and shared fixture contracts are insufficiently guarded by default checks.
evidence: pyproject.toml excludes `thenvoi-bridge/**` from pyrefly, so one production subtree is not type-checked in standard workflow.
tests/integration/conftest.py relies on a long explicit re-export list and duplicated `__all__`, which is easy to desynchronize from source fixtures.
tests/conftest.py concentrates many helper factories and fixtures used across domains, increasing blast radius for test breakage.
findings.suggestion: Add bridge to quality gates and reduce fixture indirection: include `thenvoi-bridge/**` in pyrefly scope, add a CI job for `tests/bridge` + integration fixture smoke checks, and replace manual re-export lists with a smaller explicit plugin registration strategy (`pytest_plugins`) to prevent symbol-drift errors.

findings.summary: Active production bridge code is structurally separated from package root and validation path.
evidence: Package discovery is limited to `src/thenvoi*`, while bridge production modules are in `thenvoi-bridge/bridge_core/`.
pyrefly explicitly excludes `thenvoi-bridge/**`, reinforcing separate governance for an active production directory.
README presents bridge capability in the same product narrative as SDK integrations, so current placement creates navigability and ownership ambiguity.
findings.suggestion: Run a staged reorg plan: Stage 1 create `src/thenvoi/bridge/` and move `thenvoi-bridge/bridge_core/session.py` first; Stage 2 migrate remaining bridge_core modules in dependency order (`types/session` -> `handler/router` -> `bridge/__main__`); Stage 3 update imports with `rg "thenvoi-bridge|bridge_core" -n` and targeted replacements; Stage 4 validate with `uv run ruff check .`, `uv run pyrefly check`, and `uv run pytest tests/bridge tests/integration -v`; Stage 5 remove old path and keep a temporary compatibility shim only if external tooling depends on it.

findings.summary: Contact operations are duplicated across two tool classes with no shared core.
evidence: `AgentTools` contains full contact CRUD/request methods while `ContactTools` reimplements the same API-facing transformations and validation rules.
Both classes manually build response dict payloads and metadata structures for contacts and requests, so behavior changes must be patched in multiple places.
`FakeAgentTools` mirrors the same broad method surface, increasing drift risk when contact semantics evolve.
findings.suggestion: Extract a shared contact service layer (e.g., `ContactOperations`) that owns request/response shaping and validation, then have both `AgentTools` and `ContactTools` delegate to it; keep room-bound concerns in `AgentTools` only.

findings.summary: Framework character examples duplicate the same orchestration skeleton with adapter-only differences.
evidence: Each file repeats the same flow: `load_dotenv` -> env URL checks -> `load_agent_config` -> adapter construction -> `Agent.create` -> `await agent.run()`.
The only meaningful variation is adapter type/model/prompt wiring, but lifecycle and config boilerplate is duplicated per file.
This duplication makes example updates (new required env var, startup behavior changes, diagnostics) fan out across many scripts.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a shared example bootstrap helper that centralizes env/config validation and agent startup, and let each example supply only adapter factory + scenario-specific prompt/tool configuration.

findings.summary: Per-framework `setup_logging.py` files are near-copies with small ad-hoc edits.
evidence: Each file repeats `logging.basicConfig(...)` and `thenvoi` logger setup with tiny per-framework deltas.
Framework-specific additions are encoded by manual copy changes (e.g., extra logger names or optional debug flag), not by a shared extension point.
This pattern creates consistency drift across examples for formatting, level behavior, and enabled logger namespaces.
findings.suggestion: Replace per-folder copies with one shared logging utility that accepts optional extra logger names and flags (e.g., `setup_logging(extra_loggers=[...], debug_namespaces=[...])`).

findings.summary: Runner entrypoints reimplement the same infra responsibilities instead of composing shared primitives.
evidence: Multiple runners define their own required mounts, signal handlers, retry constants/loops, and config-loading flows with similar structure.
Custom tool loading logic is repeated with mostly the same `importlib` + `TOOL_REGISTRY` pattern in more than one runner.
The shared folder already exists (`docker/shared/repo_init.py`), but runner lifecycle concerns are still split across per-runner files.
findings.suggestion: Create a shared runner-core module (startup validation, signal wiring, retry orchestration, tool loading contract) and keep each runner focused on adapter-specific configuration mapping only.

findings.summary: Sender-type casing conventions are inconsistent across formatting and gateway conversion paths.
evidence: `format_message_for_llm` treats `sender_type == "Agent"` as assistant role and passes the original sender type through without normalization.
`AgentTools.get_participants` returns participant `type` directly from API payload, again without normalization.
`GatewayHistoryConverter` records room participants only when `sender_type == "agent"` (lowercase), which is a different convention from other modules.
findings.suggestion: Normalize sender/participant type values at a single boundary (e.g., canonical enum or lowercase normalization helper) and update converters to consume only the canonical form.

findings.summary: Runner lifecycle architecture is forked into parallel framework-specific entrypoints, creating boundary drift and duplicated operational behavior.
evidence: docker/claude_sdk/runner.py and docker/codex/runner.py both implement near-identical signal setup, mount validation, retry loops, config load, and repo-init integration.
examples/claude_sdk_docker/runner.py re-implements the same bootstrap behavior with local copies of config/tool loading logic rather than consuming docker/shared helpers.
This creates three runtime bootstrap paths with slight semantic differences (required mounts, logging behavior, role prompt handling).
findings.suggestion: Introduce a shared runner core in docker/shared (for lifecycle, retry, shutdown, repo init, config merge) and keep framework runners as thin adapter-specific configuration layers. Migrate one runner first (Codex), then Claude, then example docker runner to the same core, and add a golden-path smoke test for each adapter using the shared orchestration contract.

findings.summary: Example logging setup follows inconsistent conventions across sibling framework directories.
evidence: Some setup modules force root WARNING and set namespace levels, while others set root to provided level and alter different third-party loggers.
Signature and typing are inconsistent (typed level int vs untyped defaults, optional feature flags only in one module).
Formatting conventions vary (bracket format vs dash format, stdout stream usage only in codex).
findings.suggestion: Create a single examples logging utility (for format, root policy, noise suppression, optional namespace toggles) and have per-framework setup files only pass framework-specific logger names/options. Enforce one signature: `setup_logging(level: int = logging.INFO, *, profile: str = "default") -> None`.

findings.summary: Sibling scripts handling similar startup/network failures use incompatible error contracts (raise, return, swallow).
evidence: generate_token.py raises ValueError for missing env but SystemExit for HTTP/other request failures.
01_basic_gateway.py raises ValueError when credential fallback fails; 02_with_demo_agent.py logs and returns for analogous failure path.
remote_agent.py returns []/False on broad exceptions in discovery/listing but raises RuntimeError for call failures, obscuring caller expectations.
findings.suggestion: Define a consistent script error policy: validation errors -> ValueError; operational/retriable errors -> typed runtime exception; top-level `main` decides exit code. Apply same policy to all gateway/demo/action scripts and avoid broad `except Exception` without narrowing/translation.

findings.summary: Large adapters replicate tool-wrapper scaffolding instead of using a shared tool-bridge abstraction.
evidence: crewai adapter defines many nested BaseTool classes with repeated `_report_tool_call` -> tool invocation -> `_report_tool_result` -> json response structure.
claude_sdk adapter separately implements similar tool reporting/execution mechanics via MCP tool wrappers.
Both adapters encode near-identical cross-cutting policy (reporting, success/error payload normalization) in different forms.
findings.suggestion: Extract a shared `ToolBridge` layer that takes declarative tool specs (name, schema, executor, response formatter) and emits framework-specific wrappers. Refactor crewai/claude_sdk to consume the same spec map; keep only framework binding differences local.

findings.summary: Critical startup/retry/bootstrap paths rely on manual scripts and are not covered by deterministic automated tests in this slice.
evidence: Runner files include non-trivial retries, signal handling, cancellation, and repo-init behavior but no test files are paired in this assigned set.
test_communication.py validates by sleeps and log inspection, not assertions against stable test doubles.
Failure-handling branches (retry exhaustion, signal race, repo lock timeout) are high-risk and currently hard to regress-test.
findings.suggestion: Add pytest async tests for shared runner lifecycle and repo_init helpers: (1) signal-triggered graceful shutdown, (2) retry backoff/exhaustion, (3) repo lock timeout, (4) invalid config branches. Convert `test_communication.py` into an integration test module with explicit assertions and mocked REST client/session timings.

findings.summary: Example suites contain heavy copy-paste bootstrap boilerplate, indicating accumulated AI-style template debt.
evidence: The same env var checks and ValueError strings are repeated across many files with only agent key changes.
Credential loading from `load_agent_config(...)` follows near-identical blocks in nearly every example script.
Repeated comments and scaffolding reduce signal-to-noise and increase drift risk when startup contract changes.
findings.suggestion: Introduce shared example helpers (`examples/common/bootstrap.py`) for env validation, URL defaults, and credential loading. Keep each example focused on framework behavior and scenario-specific logic only; enforce helper usage in new examples.

findings.summary: Examples package layout is overly flat and mixes scenario scripts, standalone utilities, and shared helpers in the same directories.
evidence: holistic_context.structure.directory_profiles reports `examples/langgraph/` file_count = 13, exceeding a practical flat-directory threshold while containing mixed roles.
The same structure data shows low coupling (avg_fan_in 0.0 / avg_fan_out 0.0) for most examples, indicating low-cost file moves and clear re-grouping opportunity.
`examples/coding_agents/test_communication.py` is a script-style manual verifier placed under examples with a `test_` prefix, blurring test vs example boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: Stage 1 create target folders `examples/common/`, `examples/langgraph/scenarios/`, `examples/langgraph/standalone/`, `examples/coding_agents/scripts/`; Stage 2 move files in order: shared helpers first (`setup_logging.py`, shared prompts/bootstrap), then standalone tools, then scenario scripts; Stage 3 update imports with `rg -l "from setup_logging import|import prompts" examples | xargs -I{} sed -i '' ...` (or equivalent), then validate with `uv run ruff check examples src/thenvoi/adapters docker` and `uv run pytest tests/ --ignore=tests/integration/ -v`.

findings.summary: Large adapter modules conflate framework binding, tool schema, execution orchestration, state management, and error policy in single files.
evidence: crewai.py and claude_sdk.py each exceed 1000 lines and interleave tool definitions, runtime message flow, and platform reporting concerns.
codex.py (1925 lines) indicates similar concentration of orthogonal concerns within one adapter boundary.
Repeated patterning across these files suggests structural decomposition has not kept pace with feature growth.
findings.suggestion: Split each large adapter into cohesive submodules: `tool_specs.py`, `tool_bridge.py`, `session_flow.py`, `message_pipeline.py`, `errors.py`; retain a small public adapter facade that composes these parts. Apply one adapter at a time and lock behavior with characterization tests before each extraction step.
